sections.text: In this Act unless the context otherwise requires— " Board " means the Board of the Council constituted under section 11 ; " child " has the meaning assigned to the word in the Constitution; [Cap. 1] " Council " means the National Mental Health Council established under section 8 ; " community leader " means a civil society leader, chief, headperson, minister of religion or any person of good standing in a community; " community mental health service " means a mental health service within a community; " correctional centre " means an institution where a mental patient who commits an offence is held in custody for treatment and rehabilitation ; " court " means a court of competent jurisdiction; " discrimination " has the meaning assigned to the word in the Persons with Disabilities Act, 2012; [Act No. 6 of 2012 ] " emergency " includes a situation where there is immediate and imminent danger to the health and safety of a person or others and it is demonstrated that the time required to

The following principles apply to the determination of a condition of a mental patient : the determination of whether a person is a mental patient shall be made in accordance with principles in this Act and the applicable diagnostic criteria and relevant professional standards; the determination of whether a person is a mental patient shall not be made on the basis of political, economic or social status or membership in a cultural, racial or religious group, or for any other reason not directly relevant to the mental health status of a person; a family or professional conflict, or non-conformity with moral, social, cultural or political values or religious beliefs prevailing in a person’s community, shall not be a determining factor in the diagnosis of mental illness ; and a background of past treatment or hospitalisation of a mental patient shall not be used as a basis to justify any present or future determination of mental illness .

Subject to the other provisions of this Act, a mental patient shall enjoy legal capacity. Where the nature of the mental illness , mental disorder or mental disability results in the absence of mental capacity of that mental patient , the mental patient shall not enjoy legal capacity and is legally disqualified from performing a function that requires legal capacity. Where a mental patient lacks legal capacity, a court may appoint a supporter . A mental patient who has legal capacity under subsection (1), may appoint a supporter through advance instructions. Where a court declares that a mental patient does not have legal capacity, that person is legally disqualified under subsection (4) and any other written law.
Subject to the other provisions of this Act, a mental patient shall enjoy legal capacity.

Where the nature of the mental illness , mental disorder or mental disability results in the absence of mental capacity of that mental patient , the mental patient shall not enjoy legal capacity and is legally disqualified from performing a function that requires legal capacity.
Where a mental patient lacks legal capacity, a court may appoint a supporter .
A mental patient who has legal capacity under subsection (1), may appoint a supporter through advance instructions.
Where a court declares that a mental patient does not have legal capacity, that person is legally disqualified under subsection (4) and any other written law.

A person shall not discriminate against a mental patient . A person shall not exploit or subject a mental patient to abusive, violent or degrading treatment including gender based aspects. A person shall not call a mental patient by a derogatory name on account of a disability of that mental patient .
A person shall not discriminate against a mental patient .
A person shall not exploit or subject a mental patient to abusive, violent or degrading treatment including gender based aspects.
A person shall not call a mental patient by a derogatory name on account of a disability of that mental patient .

The Minister shall, in consultation with other relevant Ministries, take policy measures to promote mental health . Without prejudice to the generality of subsection (1), the Minister shall ensure that the policy measures are aimed at— preventing or reducing the occurrence of mental illness ; enhancing awareness about mental health ; preventing or reducing stigma associated with mental illness ; training and sensitisation of law enforcement officers and adjudicators on mental health issues; ensuring the provision of adequatemental health services by— training health care providers in public health facilities and correctional centres in basic and emergency mental health care and in human rights of mental patients; necessary infrastructure provision and development; and making available finances, medical and non-medical supplies.
The Minister shall, in consultation with other relevant Ministries, take policy measures to promote mental health .

Without prejudice to the generality of subsection (1), the Minister shall ensure that the policy measures are aimed at— preventing or reducing the occurrence of mental illness ; enhancing awareness about mental health ; preventing or reducing stigma associated with mental illness ; training and sensitisation of law enforcement officers and adjudicators on mental health issues; ensuring the provision of adequatemental health services by— training health care providers in public health facilities and correctional centres in basic and emergency mental health care and in human rights of mental patients; necessary infrastructure provision and development; and making available finances, medical and non-medical supplies.

The seal of the Council shall be a device that may be determined by the Board and shall be kept by the Secretary. The affixing of the seal shall be authenticated by the Chairperson or the Vice-Chairperson and the Secretary or any other person authorised in that behalf by a resolution of the Board . A contract or instrument which, if entered into or executed by a person not being a body corporate, would not be required to be under seal, may be entered into or executed without seal on behalf of the Council by the Secretary or any other person generally or specifically authorised by the Board in that behalf. A document purporting to be a document under seal of the Council or issued on behalf of the Council shall be received in evidence and shall be deemed to be so executed or issued, without proof, unless the contrary is proved.
The seal of the Council shall be a device that may be determined by the Board and shall be kept by the Secretary.
The affixing of the seal shall be authenticated by the Chairperson or the Vice-Chairperson and the Secretary or any other person authorised in that behalf by a resolution of the Board .

A contract or instrument which, if entered into or executed by a person not being a body corporate, would not be required to be under seal, may be entered into or executed without seal on behalf of the Council by the Secretary or any other person generally or specifically authorised by the Board in that behalf.

A document purporting to be a document under seal of the Council or issued on behalf of the Council shall be received in evidence and shall be deemed to be so executed or issued, without proof, unless the contrary is proved.

The functions of the Council are to— promote and protect the rights of a mental patient ; facilitate the development of integrated, effective and efficient methods or systems of providing mental health services at all levels; collaborate with local and international organisations for the promotion of mental health ; facilitate and promote communication about mental health issues, including the elimination of stigma and discrimination against a mental patient ; facilitate the mobilisation of resources for mental health services ; advise the Minister on all policy matters related to mental health ; liaise with the body responsible for the health profession on the professional conduct and inspections of mental health facilities and correctional centres in accordance with acceptable national and international standards; liaise with Zambia Agency for Persons with Disabilities and relevant institutions on mental health related programmes; develop guidelines for special and intrusive treatmen

There is constituted a Board of the Council which consists of the following part-time members appointed by the Minister: one representative each of the ministries responsible for— correctional services; social welfare; health; finance; and local government; a representative of the Attorney-General; a representative of the Administrator-General and Official Receiver; a representative of the Human Rights Commission; a representative of the General Nursing Council of Zambia; a representative of the Health Professions Council of Zambia; a representative of a relevant non-governmental organisation dealing with mental health issues; a Psychiatrist; and one other person from the community with experience and knowledge in mental health issues. The Minister shall appoint the Chairperson of the Board from among the members who are not public officers. The Members shall elect the Vice-Chairperson of the Board from among themselves. A person is not qualified to be appointed as a member if that per

There is constituted a Board of the Council which consists of the following part-time members appointed by the Minister: one representative each of the ministries responsible for— correctional services; social welfare; health; finance; and local government; a representative of the Attorney-General; a representative of the Administrator-General and Official Receiver; a representative of the Human Rights Commission; a representative of the General Nursing Council of Zambia; a representative of the Health Professions Council of Zambia; a representative of a relevant non-governmental organisation dealing with mental health issues; a Psychiatrist; and one other person from the community with experience and knowledge in mental health issues.
The Minister shall appoint the Chairperson of the Board from among the members who are not public officers.
The Members shall elect the Vice-Chairperson of the Board from among themselves.

A person is not qualified to be appointed as a member if that person— is not a citizen of Zambia; is an undischarged bankrupt; is legally disqualified from performing the functions of a member; has been convicted of an offence under any written law and sentenced to imprisonment for a period exceeding six months without the option of a fine within a period of five years preceding the appointment; has been found guilty of professional misconduct; or is an employee of the Council .
The provisions of the Schedule apply to the Board .

Subject to this Act, the functions of the Board are to— review the policy and strategic plan of the Council ; oversee the implementation and successful operation of the policy and functions of the Council ; approve the annual budget and plans of the Council ; monitor and evaluate the performance of the Council against budgets and plans; and establish and approve rules and procedures for the appointment, discipline, termination and terms and conditions of service of the staff of the Council .

The Board shall appoint the Executive Director who shall be the chief executive officer of the Council on the terms and conditions that the Board may determine with the approval of the Emoluments Commission. The Executive Director shall, subject to the control of the Board , be responsible for the day-to-day administration of the Council . A person shall not be appointed as the Executive Director unless that person is a psychiatrist . The Executive Director shall attend meetings of the Board and any committee of the Board , and may address that meeting, but shall not vote on any matter. The Board may, appoint, on the terms and conditions that the Board may determine with the approval of the Emoluments Commission, other staff that the Board considers necessary for the performance of the functions of the Council .

The Board shall appoint the Executive Director who shall be the chief executive officer of the Council on the terms and conditions that the Board may determine with the approval of the Emoluments Commission.
The Executive Director shall, subject to the control of the Board , be responsible for the day-to-day administration of the Council .
A person shall not be appointed as the Executive Director unless that person is a psychiatrist .
The Executive Director shall attend meetings of the Board and any committee of the Board , and may address that meeting, but shall not vote on any matter.

The Board may, appoint, on the terms and conditions that the Board may determine with the approval of the Emoluments Commission, other staff that the Board considers necessary for the performance of the functions of the Council .

A mental health facility shall put in place appropriate measures to ensure— availability of mental health services at all levels of mental health care ; adequate financial and geographical accessibility; provision of services that meet prescribed minimum standards; and access to psychiatric medication. Mental health services shall be provided on an equal basis with physical health services where possible. Mental health services shall— be integrated within the general health services; be provided in specialised public and private health facilities; be provided indesignated health facilities for forensic mental patients; promote in mental patient and out mental patient services at community level; promote the rights and interests of mental patients; promote and improve the mental health status of the population; and provide primary health care . A health practitioner who is not a psychiatrist shall not cause a mental patient to receive prescribed psychiatric medication for more than six

A mental health facility shall put in place appropriate measures to ensure— availability of mental health services at all levels of mental health care ; adequate financial and geographical accessibility; provision of services that meet prescribed minimum standards; and access to psychiatric medication.
Mental health services shall be provided on an equal basis with physical health services where possible.

Mental health services shall— be integrated within the general health services; be provided in specialised public and private health facilities; be provided indesignated health facilities for forensic mental patients; promote in mental patient and out mental patient services at community level; promote the rights and interests of mental patients; promote and improve the mental health status of the population; and provide primary health care .

A health practitioner who is not a psychiatrist shall not cause a mental patient to receive prescribed psychiatric medication for more than six months without being authorised by a psychiatrist who is designated to provide medication and review psychiatric treatment .
A health practitioner shall provide mental health services in a manner that facilitates the involvement of the community, family members and support persons.

A head of a mental health facility or institution or any other person delegated with the duties under this Part shall follow the prescribed exemption criteria for categories of mental patients eligible for free health services at public health facilities.

In prescribing the exemption criteria referred to under subsection (6), regard shall be given to the— range of free mental health services currently available; categories of mental patients already receiving free mental health services ; therapeutic impact to mental patients; and needs of groups such as women, children, elderly persons, inmates, other persons with disabilities and persons receiving compensation for compensable occupational diseases.

A mental patient has the right to— effective, timely, safe, considerate and respectful care and support; obtain frommental health practitioners appropriate, current and understandable information about diagnosis, treatment and prognosis; discuss and request information about the specific procedures or treatments, the risks involved, the possible length of recuperation and the medically reasonable alternatives and their risks and benefits; know the identity of the mental health practitioner involved in the care of the mental health services user; make decisions about the plan of care before and during treatment ; refuse a recommended treatment or plan of care to the extent allowed by law and to be informed of the medical consequences of that refusal; alternative appropriate care and services provided by the hospital or be transferred to another hospital in the case of a refusal of recommended treatment or plan of care; be notified by the hospital of any policy that might affect choice o

Subject to the other provisions of this Act, a mental patient shall— provide information relating to mental illness including mental health interventions; provide information relating to the next of kin where a mental patient will not be in a position to provide information; participate effectively in decision making and request additional information or explanation about the health status or treatment when they do not fully understand information and instructions; and provide information on mode of payment for mental health services .

A mental health facility or correctional centre shall take steps to ensure that, the mental patient — is accorded privacy and confidentiality relating to information obtained in the therapeutic relationship, and that the information given shall only be used for the purpose of improving the mental patient ’s mental health ; is protected from exploitation, abuse or any cruel or degrading treatment ; is informed when personal information is passed on to a third party; and has unrestricted access to information concerning that mental patient ’s mental health care , treatment , rehabilitation and palliation.

A mental health facility or correctional centre shall take steps to ensure that, the mental patient — is accorded privacy and confidentiality relating to information obtained in the therapeutic relationship, and that the information given shall only be used for the purpose of improving the mental patient ’s mental health ; is protected from exploitation, abuse or any cruel or degrading treatment ; is informed when personal information is passed on to a third party; and has unrestricted access to information concerning that mental patient ’s mental health care , treatment , rehabilitation and palliation.

A mental patient or forensic mental patient shall receive mental health services which is appropriate for that patient’s needs in accordance with relevant health service standards. A mental patient or forensic mental patient shall be protected from harm, inappropriate medication, or acts causing mental distress or physical discomfort and abuse by other mental patients, staff, family members or any other person. A mental patient or forensic mental patient shall be treated in the least restrictive environment and with the least restrictive or intrusive treatment appropriate to the mental patient ’s health needs and the need to protect the physical safety of the mental patient or any other person. Despite subsection (3), a mental health facility may treat a mental patient or forensic mental patient in a more restrictive environment for a period required to give effect to the appropriate, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation and palliation. The treatment of a mental patient or forensi
A mental patient or forensic mental patient shall receive mental health services which is appropriate for that patient’s needs in accordance with relevant health service standards.

A mental patient or forensic mental patient shall be protected from harm, inappropriate medication, or acts causing mental distress or physical discomfort and abuse by other mental patients, staff, family members or any other person.

A mental patient or forensic mental patient shall be treated in the least restrictive environment and with the least restrictive or intrusive treatment appropriate to the mental patient ’s health needs and the need to protect the physical safety of the mental patient or any other person.

Despite subsection (3), a mental health facility may treat a mental patient or forensic mental patient in a more restrictive environment for a period required to give effect to the appropriate, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation and palliation.
The treatment of a mental patient or forensic mental patient shall be directed towards restoring and enhancing personal autonomy.

A health facility shall, on admission, inform a mental patient or forensic mental patient of the— rights of that mental patient or forensic mental patient , in a form and language which a mental patient or forensic mental patient understands; and health care standards which the mental patient or forensic mental patient is entitled to receive. Where a mental patient or forensic mental patient is unable to understand the information communicated under subsection (1), that information shall be communicated to a supporter .

A health facility shall, on admission, inform a mental patient or forensic mental patient of the— rights of that mental patient or forensic mental patient , in a form and language which a mental patient or forensic mental patient understands; and health care standards which the mental patient or forensic mental patient is entitled to receive.
Where a mental patient or forensic mental patient is unable to understand the information communicated under subsection (1), that information shall be communicated to a supporter .

A mental health facility shall meet the following minimum standards for the provision of the mental health services : provide a qualified psychiatrist or a mental health practitioner and other appropriate professional staff; have adequate space; implement an appropriate and active therapy programme; avail and maintain medical and non-medical equipment, diagnostic and therapeutic equipment, kitchen, laundry and mortuary; provide information communication technology, security systems and equipment, and transport; and ensure adequate and consistent medical and nonmedical supplies. A mental health facility shall be inspected as provided for under the Health Professions Act, 2009, the Nurses and Midwives Act, 1997, and the Medicines and Allied Substances Act, 2013. [Act No. 24 of 2009; Act No. 31 of 1997 ; Act No. 3 of 2013 ]

A mental health facility shall be inspected as provided for under the Health Professions Act, 2009, the Nurses and Midwives Act, 1997, and the Medicines and Allied Substances Act, 2013. [Act No. 24 of 2009; Act No. 31 of 1997 ; Act No. 3 of 2013 ]

A mental health facility shall only provide treatment , care, support, rehabilitation and palliation services to, or admit a mental patient where— the mental patient or a supporter has consented to the admission, treatment , care, rehabilitation or palliation; the Board authorises the admission, treatment care, rehabilitationor palliation; the admission, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services if not done may result in— death or irreversible harm to a mental patient ; a mental patient inflicting serious harm to oneself or another person; or a mental patient causing serious damage to, or loss of, property. A mental health facility that provides admission, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services under subsection (1)(c)— shall render a report, in writing, to the Council in the prescribed manner; and may only provide the admission, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services for a period of seventy-two hours unless an a

A mental health facility shall only provide treatment , care, support, rehabilitation and palliation services to, or admit a mental patient where— the mental patient or a supporter has consented to the admission, treatment , care, rehabilitation or palliation; the Board authorises the admission, treatment care, rehabilitationor palliation; the admission, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services if not done may result in— death or irreversible harm to a mental patient ; a mental patient inflicting serious harm to oneself or another person; or a mental patient causing serious damage to, or loss of, property.

A mental health facility that provides admission, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services under subsection (1)(c)— shall render a report, in writing, to the Council in the prescribed manner; and may only provide the admission, treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services for a period of seventy-two hours unless an application is made to the Board within that period for the provision of services or admission for a longer period.
A mental health practitioner shall minimise admission, treatment , care, support rehabilitation or palliation services without informed consent .

Where a mental patient requires special treatment which cannot be administered routinely to the mental patient even with the mental patient ’s consent, three medical doctors, one of whom shall be a psychiatrist shall certify that the person concerned is capable of understanding the nature, purpose and likely effects of the treatment .

Where a mental patient is unable to give consent to the treatment , the consent may be given by a supporter . Where the mental patient is below the age of eighteen years the consent may be made by the mental patient ’s parent or guardian.
Where a mental patient is unable to give consent to the treatment , the consent may be given by a supporter .
Where the mental patient is below the age of eighteen years the consent may be made by the mental patient ’s parent or guardian.

A mental patient who understands the mental patients right to decide on an option of treatment shall state in advance the mental patient ’s choice of treatment . An advance decision may be in writing and signed by the mental patient , a recorded oral statement or a note of a particular discussion recorded on the mental patient ’s file, except that where a mental patient ’s advance decision relates to a refusal of lifeprolonging treatment , this shall be recorded in writing. An advance decision shall be either a— statement authorising or requesting specific procedures; or clear instruction refusing some or all medical procedures. An advance decision shall be legally binding where the mental patient is an adult and competent to make an informed decision . Where a mental patient subsequently grants a person a power of attorney to make a decision, an advance decision which was made by the mental patient ceases to have effect.
A mental patient who understands the mental patients right to decide on an option of treatment shall state in advance the mental patient ’s choice of treatment .

An advance decision may be in writing and signed by the mental patient , a recorded oral statement or a note of a particular discussion recorded on the mental patient ’s file, except that where a mental patient ’s advance decision relates to a refusal of lifeprolonging treatment , this shall be recorded in writing.
An advance decision shall be either a— statement authorising or requesting specific procedures; or clear instruction refusing some or all medical procedures.
An advance decision shall be legally binding where the mental patient is an adult and competent to make an informed decision .
Where a mental patient subsequently grants a person a power of attorney to make a decision, an advance decision which was made by the mental patient ceases to have effect.

A mental health facility shall where a person requires treatment at a mental health facility , endeavour to ensure that— there is voluntary admission ; the procedure for admission to a mental health facility is consistent with a hospital treating physical health; and while admission or treatment on a voluntary basis shall be the preferred practice, involuntary admission or treatment , shall be provided where they are necessary for the health and safety of the mental patient . A person on voluntary admission , treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services may leave the mental health facility at any time in accordance with the prescribed regulations. An involuntary admission shall be carried out as prescribed.

A mental health facility shall where a person requires treatment at a mental health facility , endeavour to ensure that— there is voluntary admission ; the procedure for admission to a mental health facility is consistent with a hospital treating physical health; and while admission or treatment on a voluntary basis shall be the preferred practice, involuntary admission or treatment , shall be provided where they are necessary for the health and safety of the mental patient .
A person on voluntary admission , treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation services may leave the mental health facility at any time in accordance with the prescribed regulations.
An involuntary admission shall be carried out as prescribed.

A special treatment such as electro-convulsive therapy or psycho-surgery shall be— provided in accordance with written instructions by a psychiatrist ; and applied under the authorisation and supervision of a consultant psychiatrist . Seclusion and restraint shall— be provided under an authorisation and supervision of a consultant psychiatrist ; not exceed seventy-two hours unless an application is made to the Board within that period for the seclusion or restraint for a longer period; and be undertaken with intermittent reviews. A psychiatrist shall, after the period of seclusion and restraint referred to under subsection (2) (b), prepare a report to the Board on the mental health status of the mental patient and may recommend release from, or extension of, the seclusion or restraint.

A special treatment such as electro-convulsive therapy or psycho-surgery shall be— provided in accordance with written instructions by a psychiatrist ; and applied under the authorisation and supervision of a consultant psychiatrist .

Seclusion and restraint shall— be provided under an authorisation and supervision of a consultant psychiatrist ; not exceed seventy-two hours unless an application is made to the Board within that period for the seclusion or restraint for a longer period; and be undertaken with intermittent reviews.

A psychiatrist shall, after the period of seclusion and restraint referred to under subsection (2) (b), prepare a report to the Board on the mental health status of the mental patient and may recommend release from, or extension of, the seclusion or restraint.

Where a court issues an order for a forensic mental patient to be admitted for mental health services , the Registrar or the Clerk of the court shall send a copy of that order to a designated health facility . A designated health facility shall examine the forensic mental patient within fourteen days of the date of the court order.

Where a court issues an order for a forensic mental patient to be admitted for mental health services , the Registrar or the Clerk of the court shall send a copy of that order to a designated health facility .
A designated health facility shall examine the forensic mental patient within fourteen days of the date of the court order.

Where a mental health practitioner is of the opinion that it would be for the benefit of a forensic mental patient admitted in a designated health facility , or that it is necessary for the purpose of obtaining special treatment for the forensic mental patient , transfer the forensic mental patient to another designated health facility after obtaining consent from the designated health facility where the forensic patient is being transferred to. In the case of a forensic patient under a correctional centre , consent shallbe obtained fromthe Commissioner-General of Zambia Correctional Service.

Where a mental health practitioner is of the opinion that it would be for the benefit of a forensic mental patient admitted in a designated health facility , or that it is necessary for the purpose of obtaining special treatment for the forensic mental patient , transfer the forensic mental patient to another designated health facility after obtaining consent from the designated health facility where the forensic patient is being transferred to.
In the case of a forensic patient under a correctional centre , consent shallbe obtained fromthe Commissioner-General of Zambia Correctional Service.

The in-charge of a designated health facility shall ensure that the forensic mental patient is reviewed every six months by a psychiatrist , and submit a report to the Minister responsible for correctional services, in the prescribed manner and form. The review under subsection (1), shall include recommendations on— a plan for further treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation service of a forensic mental patient ; or the discharge of a forensic mental patient from a designated health facility .

The in-charge of a designated health facility shall ensure that the forensic mental patient is reviewed every six months by a psychiatrist , and submit a report to the Minister responsible for correctional services, in the prescribed manner and form.

The review under subsection (1), shall include recommendations on— a plan for further treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation service of a forensic mental patient ; or the discharge of a forensic mental patient from a designated health facility .

Where an officer-in-charge of a correctional centre observes or receives information that an inmate may be mentally ill, the officer-in charge of the correctional centre shall cause the mental health status of the inmate to be examined within forty-eight hours— by a psychiatrist ; or where a psychiatrist is not readily available, by— a medical practitioner; or a mental health care practitioner. The person conducting the assessment under subsection (1) shall submit a written report to the officer-in-charge of the correctional centre , within fourteen days of the assessment and shall specify in the report— the mental health status of the inmate; and a plan for the treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation of that inmate.

Where an officer-in-charge of a correctional centre observes or receives information that an inmate may be mentally ill, the officer-in charge of the correctional centre shall cause the mental health status of the inmate to be examined within forty-eight hours— by a psychiatrist ; or where a psychiatrist is not readily available, by— a medical practitioner; or a mental health care practitioner.

The person conducting the assessment under subsection (1) shall submit a written report to the officer-in-charge of the correctional centre , within fourteen days of the assessment and shall specify in the report— the mental health status of the inmate; and a plan for the treatment , care, support, rehabilitation or palliation of that inmate.

Where a mental health practitioner conducting the assessment referred to in section 34 , finds that the mental illness of an inmate is of a nature that requires the inmate to be treated and cared for in a designated health facility referred to in section 22 , the officer-in-charge of the correctional centre shall, within forty-eight hours, cause the inmate to be transferred to a mental health facility. Despite subsection (1), where the mental health practitioner recommends that an inmate or unconvicted inmate may be treated, cared for, supported, rehabilitated or palliated at a designated health facility , the mental health practitioner shall recommend to the officer-in-charge of the correctional centre the transfer of that inmate or unconvicted inmate to the designated health facility .

Where a mental health practitioner conducting the assessment referred to in section 34 , finds that the mental illness of an inmate is of a nature that requires the inmate to be treated and cared for in a designated health facility referred to in section 22 , the officer-in-charge of the correctional centre shall, within forty-eight hours, cause the inmate to be transferred to a mental health facility.

Despite subsection (1), where the mental health practitioner recommends that an inmate or unconvicted inmate may be treated, cared for, supported, rehabilitated or palliated at a designated health facility , the mental health practitioner shall recommend to the officer-in-charge of the correctional centre the transfer of that inmate or unconvicted inmate to the designated health facility .

The head of a mental health facility in which a mentally ill inmate is admitted shall cause the mental health status of the mentally ill inmate to be reviewed, and prepare and submit a report to the officer-in-charge of the relevant correctional centre . The review shall— specify the mental health status of the mentally ill inmate; and set out recommendations regarding a plan for further treatment , care and rehabilitation services for the mentally ill inmate.

The head of a mental health facility in which a mentally ill inmate is admitted shall cause the mental health status of the mentally ill inmate to be reviewed, and prepare and submit a report to the officer-in-charge of the relevant correctional centre .

The review shall— specify the mental health status of the mentally ill inmate; and set out recommendations regarding a plan for further treatment , care and rehabilitation services for the mentally ill inmate.

The Minister may, by statutory instrument in consultation with the Council , make regulations to give effect to the purposes and provisions of this Act. Without limiting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister may make regulations for the— forms and certificates to be used for the purposes of the Act; guidelines for the establishment, development, maintenance and management of mental health facilities; exemption criteria for categories of mental patients eligible for free health services at public facilities; procedure and form for foreign mental health service users or mental patients coming to Zambia and mental health service users seeking treatment abroad including foreigners who are either on short or long stay, the transfer of mental patients between designated health facilities; after care services, support, rehabilitation and palliation programmes for mental health service users and mental patients; establishment of transit homes and boarding home care services; de-instit
The Minister may, by statutory instrument in consultation with the Council , make regulations to give effect to the purposes and provisions of this Act.

Without limiting the generality of subsection (1), the Minister may make regulations for the— forms and certificates to be used for the purposes of the Act; guidelines for the establishment, development, maintenance and management of mental health facilities; exemption criteria for categories of mental patients eligible for free health services at public facilities; procedure and form for foreign mental health service users or mental patients coming to Zambia and mental health service users seeking treatment abroad including foreigners who are either on short or long stay, the transfer of mental patients between designated health facilities; after care services, support, rehabilitation and palliation programmes for mental health service users and mental patients; establishment of transit homes and boarding home care services; de-institutionalisation of mental patients; and administration of special treatments.

On the commencement of this Act— an order made under the repealed Act that is still in force shall continue as if made under this Act; an estate and property of a mental patient that was being administered under the repealed Act shall continue to be administered as if the order for the administration of the estate or property was made under this Act; a mental patient under an order of detention pending removal shall be deemed to be lawfully detained under this Act; an institution established for the reception, treatment or detention of a mental patient shall continue to exist as if established under this Act; wherever in any written law a reference to a lunatic or to lunacy, to an asylum or similar terms are contained, that reference shall be read and construed as a reference to a mental patient or similar terms, within the meaning of this Act; and wherever in any written law a reference to mental disability or legal disqualification, or similar terms are contained, that reference shal
